etymology.description: Depicts sugarcane with full stems -> used phonetically for abstract meanings
etymology.interpretation: Originally, 者 depicted a sugarcane, emphasizing its full stems and possibly depicting drops of liquid or leaves. This pictogram was visually and phonetically linked to either the words for 'sugarcane' or 'to boil.' Over time, it was borrowed phonetically for more abstract meanings relating to 'one who' or the agentive '-er/-ist.'

examples.chinese: 读者需要安静的环境(讀者需要安靜的環境)。
作者正在创作新小说(作者正在創作新小說)。
examples.english: The author is creating a new novel.
